channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Government services continue after Mthatha Botha Sigcau building fire, says Eastern Cape Premier
item.description: Government services in the Eastern Cape are continuing despite the severe fire that damaged the iconic Botha Sigcau building in Mthatha on 24 March 2026. The building, once the headquarters of the for...

item.title: DCEA intensifies anti-drug crackdown, seizes nearly two tonnes of narcotics across Tanzania
item.description: The Drug Control and Enforcement Authority (DCEA) of Tanzania has seized almost two tonnes of illegal drugs and arrested 77 suspects following a nationwide crackdown in March. Commissioner General Are...

item.title: MMDA prepares measures for expected 3-day transport strike affecting commuters
item.description: The Metropolitan Manila Development Authority (MMDA) has announced its preparedness for an anticipated three-day transport strike that is expected to affect commuters in Metro Manila. In response to t...

item.title: Kochi Biennale Showcases Powerful Art Inspired by Ambedkar and Anti-Caste Movements
item.description: The Kochi-Muziris Biennale recently concluded, leaving behind a poignant display of artwork deeply rooted in Ambedkarite philosophy and anti-caste movements. The exhibit highlighted the enduring strug...

item.title: Elon Musk disputes South Africa Starlink licence denial over B-BBEE ownership requirements
item.description: Elon Musk don publicly accuse South African government of discrimination after his satellite internet company, Starlink, no fit secure operating licence for the country. According to Musk, the refusal...

item.title: Trump’s Conflict with the Pope Sparks Backlash Among Religious Supporters
item.description: Donald Trump has ignited a new controversy with the Catholic Church by publicly attacking Pope Leo XIV, the first American Pope, who criticized the U.S. war in Iran. Trump labelled the Pope as 'weak o...

item.title: Twelve police recruits in Nairobi charged over alleged KCSE certificate forgery
item.description: Twelve police recruits in Nairobi have been charged in court over allegations of forging Kenya Certificate of Secondary Education (KCSE) documents in an attempt to secure jobs in the Kenya Police Serv...

item.title: Analysis of CAG Report: Economic Impact and Effectiveness of Recommended Remedies
item.description: The article discusses the economic importance of the Controller and Auditor General (CAG) report in Tanzania, highlighting how it functions as a key tool for assessing public financial management and ...

item.title: Delhi High Court reserves verdict on Kejriwal’s plea seeking recusal of Justice Swarana Kanta Sharma in excise policy case
item.description: The Delhi High Court has reserved its judgement on a plea filed by former Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal seeking the recusal of Justice Swarana Kanta Sharma from hearing the Central Bureau of In...

item.title: Nightclub operator urges ACT government to address rising costs and regulatory pressures on Canberra nightlife
item.description: A prominent Canberra nightclub owner has criticised the ACT government for what they describe as a lack of meaningful engagement with the challenges facing the city’s night-time economy. Speaking anon...

item.title: Elima Chesang: Five decades of performing for Kenya’s presidents and struggling in old age
item.description: Elima Chesang, a 74-year-old traditional singer and performer from Baringo North, has spent over five decades entertaining Kenya’s top leadership, including all five presidents from Jomo Kenyatta to W...

item.title: Golden Knights defeat Jets 6–2; Winnipeg eliminated from playoff contention
item.description: The Vegas Golden Knights secured a decisive 6–2 victory over the Winnipeg Jets in a game that had significant implications for the NHL playoff race. According to a report from The Associated Press, ke...

item.title: Deputy defence official defends actions after ethics finding on hiring process tied to diversity objectives
item.description: Christiane Fox, the deputy minister at Canada’s Department of National Defence, told a parliamentary committee that she does not regret actions that a federal ethics investigation found violated confl...

item.title: Nyerere’s unity message remains vital for Tanzania’s stability
item.description: The article reflects on the enduring relevance of Mwalimu Julius Nyerere’s call for unity, peace, and national cohesion in Tanzania, especially as the nation commemorates what would have been his 104t...

item.title: Teenager bailed after arrest over arson attack on Jewish ambulance service in north London
item.description: A 19-year-old man arrested in connection with an arson attack on ambulances belonging to a Jewish volunteer emergency service in north London has been released on bail. The Metropolitan Police confirm...

item.title: Admiral Naveed Ashraf affirms Pakistan’s defence readiness and highlights military progress
item.description: The Chief of Naval Staff of Pakistan, Admiral Naveed Ashraf, reaffirmed on Tuesday that Pakistan’s armed forces are prepared to deter any aggression and deliver a firm response if required. Speaking a...

item.title: LAUSD schools to remain open after tentative agreements with unions avert strike
item.description: The Los Angeles Unified School District (LAUSD) will remain open on Tuesday after reaching a series of tentative agreements with three major employee unions, effectively averting a potential strike. L...

item.title: A former children’s entertainer reflects on the joy of working with kids and the challenges posed by parents
item.description: The article is a personal reflection from a former children’s entertainer who worked between the ages of 16 and 22, performing at parties as a fairy and other themed characters such as witches, prince...

item.title: PM Shehbaz to visit Saudi Arabia, Qatar and Turkiye for diplomatic talks amid regional tensions
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif of Pakistan is expected to undertake an important three-nation diplomatic visit covering Saudi Arabia, Qatar, and Turkiye, as the government steps up engagement amid esca...
